summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-encrypted Windows binary from GitHub
details: The package's postinstall hook (install-hook.js) executes an obfuscated PowerShell command via `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <base64>` that downloads https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin, XOR-decrypts the bytes with key 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, runs it hidden via Start-Process, and deletes the file. After dropping the payload, the script writes a cleanup_*.bat to %TEMP% that waits 30 seconds and recursively rmdir's the package directory, and rewrites the parent project's package.json to remove `mailconfirmer` from dependencies/devDependencies — anti-forensic behavior intended to hide the dropper from the developer. The advertised library surface (`sendConfirmationEmail`, `verifyToken` in index.js) is a non-functional stub that only console.logs; the package has no legitimate function and exists solely as a delivery vector. The download source is a personal GitHub account (Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note) unrelated to the package's stated purpose, and the fetched bytes are an opaque XOR-obfuscated Windows executable.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
